learning_outcomes.description: Implement effective verbal, non-verbal, and written communication strategies to convey physiotherapy knowledge clearly and appropriately to clients and interprofessional health care teams in diverse clinical, cultural, and lifespan contexts.
Plan and implement safe, systematic, and efficient assessments of clients within acute and chronic cardiorespiratory contexts.
Apply clinical reasoning to select physiotherapy interventions for acute and chronic cardiorespiratory conditions across the lifespan, and explain the underlying theory and rationale.

Develop and deliver safe, effective, and evidence-based physiotherapy interventions that are holistic, and prioritised according to assessment findings and client goals, including delegation and referral where appropriate.
Evaluate the effectiveness of cardiorespiratory physiotherapy using appropriate outcome measures, re-evaluation, and record keeping.

assessments.due_date:
9/09/2026

Please see the Assessment tab on Blackboard for information about this assessment item.
assessments.task_description:
Please see the Assessment tab on Blackboard for information about this assessment item.

Viva examination (clinical conversation with a clinical educator, in a simulated clinical environment) + written examination component. The examinable materials include all content and related knowledge from Module 1 (including lectures, practicals, ILP, CALs, case discussion sessions, SP sessions). Principles covered in Module 1 learning activities should underpin your knowledge and approach to responding to questions (for example but not limited to, how you: prepare to assess a patient, perform assessment, outline clinical reasoning, prepare for management, reassess using outcome measures, modify your approach to suit the patient, plan for follow-up, communicate with members of the team, etc).

Feedback: Overall, summary of feedback will be provided, including cohort feedback on areas for further development. This will be released after deferred examinations are complete. Requests for further feedback must be submitted in writing to the course coordinator, and must include a reflection on exam performance and specific questions for feedback.

In this course the recording of an assessment (oral/practical) performance has been waived, a record of student performance will be taken by other means such as detailed written notes that can be referred to if a remark is required at a later date.

Use of AI technology: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please see the Assessment tab on Blackboard for information about this assessment item.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date:
21/10/2026

Submit to the Educator at the start of your usual practical class, for peer examination during class.
assessments.task_description:
Please see the Assessment tab on Blackboard for information about this assessment item.

Workbook activities related to simulation cases, submitted for assessment (peer examination). 

*Students can only participate in the peer examination process (and thus be eligible to receive 5%) if they submit a Workbook. Instructions for this item and the peer examination process will be available in the Assessment tab on Blackboard.

Feedback: Individual feedback on this item will be provided via the peer examination process. 

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit hardcopy of Workbook to the Educator at the start of your usual practical class, for peer examination during class.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

7/11/2026 - 21/11/2026

assessments.task_description:
Please see the Assessment tab on Blackboard for further information about this assessment item.

Viva examination (clinical conversation with a clinical educator, in a simulated clinical environment) during the Central Examination period. The examinable materials include all content and related knowledge in Module 1 and Module 2. Principles covered in Module 1 and Module 2 learning activities should underpin your knowledge and approach to responding to questions (for example but not limited to, how you: prepare to assess a patient, perform assessment, outline clinical reasoning, prepare for management, reassess using outcome measures, modify your approach to suit the patient, plan for follow-up, communicate with members of the team, etc).

This is a MUST PASS component of the course (≥55%)

This examination will be scheduled on-campus in a face-to-face format, during the central examination period. 

Feedback: Overall, summary of feedback will be provided via Blackboard, including cohort feedback on areas for further development. This will be released after deferred examinations are complete. Requests for further feedback must be submitted in writing to the course coordinator, and must include a reflection on exam performance and specific questions for feedback. Additional feedback will be provided within 14 calendar days. End-semester exam marks will not be released until after the release of the final grade.

In this course the recording of an assessment (oral/practical) performance has been waived, a record of student performance will be taken by other means such as detailed written notes that can be referred to if a remark is required at a later date.

Use of AI technology: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Practical assessment during the Central Examination period. The examinable techniques include all practical content and related knowledge from Module 1 and Module 2 (excluding the specifics related to the paediatric mannequins). 

This is a MUST PASS component of the course (≥55%)

Practical examinations will be scheduled on-campus in a face-to-face format, during the central examination period. 

Feedback: Overall, summary of feedback will be provided via Blackboard, including cohort feedback on areas for further development. This will be released after deferred examinations are complete. Requests for further feedback must be submitted in writing to the course coordinator, and must include a reflection on exam performance and specific questions for feedback. Additional feedback will be provided within 14 calendar days. End-semester practical exam marks will not be released until after the release of the final grade.

In this course the recording of an assessment (oral/practical) performance has been waived, a record of student performance will be taken by other means such as detailed written notes that can be referred to if a remark is required at a later date.

Use of AI technology: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 7(27 Jul - 13 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 1

Cardiorespiratory Physiotherapy Assessment and Management in Respiratory and Cardiac settings.

Lectures are pre-recorded for self-paced coverage (along with other independent learning activities) prior to related in-person practicals and clinical simulations each week. There is a weekly average of 2h of pre-recorded lectures + 2h of other independent learning activities across semester.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

There is a weekly average of 2h of pre-recorded lectures + 2h of other independent learning activities across semester.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 7(27 Jul - 13 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 1

Cardiorespiratory Physiotherapy Assessment and Management in Respiratory and Cardiac settings.

Practicals (including clinical simulations) are 2h each week (see timetable). Prior to participating in these sessions, students must complete the associated self-paced online independent learning tasks (pre-recorded lectures, guided "how to" skills videos, ILPs, CALs, case reading). Practicals and clinical simulations are experiential learning activities, and participants are expected to attend and actively engage; given the interactive nature, these sessions won't be recorded.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
